subject: Biology
title: Germ Theory: A Revolution
description: A short lesson to help you apply Germ Theory: A Revolution.

hook.text: Before the 1860s, doctors didn't wash their hands between patients. Infections were a mystery.

buildup.text: Louis Pasteur showed that microbes cause fermentation and spoilage—not spontaneous generation.

discovery.text: Robert Koch proved specific bacteria cause specific diseases, establishing Koch's postulates.

twist.text: When Semmelweis urged hand-washing in hospitals, fellow doctors ridiculed and rejected him.

climax.text: Germ theory transformed medicine. Surgery, sanitation, and vaccines all followed from this idea.

punchline.text: Invisible germs were killing millions all along.

quiz.question: Who established that specific bacteria cause specific diseases?
